message:
fix(server): throttle full integrity checks on attempt time, not success time

The integrity guard was only updating last_full_attempt when the full
check cycle succeeded. A persistently failing full check would be
retried on every monitor_loop iteration (every few seconds), wasting
I/O on a check that is likely to keep failing until the underlying
issue is resolved.

Changed to record the attempt timestamp before running the full cycle,
so both successful and failed attempts throttle the next run equally.
This prevents tight-loop retries while still respecting the configured
integrity_check_interval_hours.

Added test full_check_due_uses_last_attempt_not_last_success() verifying
that a stale success triggers a new attempt but a recent failed attempt
properly throttles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
